action.reasoning: Observation: Iter 8 training crashed with NaN loss at step 1, likely due to unstable ViT-L initialization with lr=2e-5 and AMP. Iter 6 ViT-B baseline achieved 0.3999 worst-region (Africa worst). Analysis: ViT-L is higher capacity but training instability wastes iteration. Better approach: stick with ViT-B (stable), add Group DRO (Distributed Robust Optimization) to directly optimize worst-region accuracy by upweighting high-loss regions, along with class-weighted sampling for class imbalance. Group DRO is proven for WILDS tasks. Decision: rewrite train.py with Group DRO, strong augmentation, 4-GPU DataParallel, 20 epochs, log per-region val accuracy, save best checkpoint based on worst-region val accuracy. Then submit eval.
